name: Tirimmah ibn Adi
title: Compassionate Guide
shortDescription: A sincere Shia who guided the Imam's caravan and left to bring provisions and troops, but heard the news of the Imam's martyrdom upon his return.
fullStory:
Tirimmah ibn Adi al-Ta'i was a brave and eloquent Shia. Midway from Mecca to Kufa, he and a group of his companions met Imam Hussain (AS) and he took on the responsibility of guiding the Imam's caravan through the desert routes.

Tirimmah suggested to the Imam to take refuge in the safe mountains of his tribe (Tayy), but the Imam declined. Tirimmah then asked for permission to deliver provisions to his family and bring back the warriors of his tribe, promising to return swiftly.

The Imam permitted him, saying: 'If you intend to return, make haste.' Tirimmah left quickly, but due to the long distance and travel difficulties, his return was delayed.

When he and a group of men from his tribe were on their way back to Karbala, he received the shocking news of Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom mid-journey. Having missed the opportunity to help the Imam, he wept in regret over this delay for the rest of his life and remained loyal to the Ahl al-Bayt.
quoteArabic: يا حُسَيْنُ! إِنِّي أَرى قَوْماً قَلِيلًا، وَلَوْ لَمْ يُقاتِلْكَ إِلّا هؤُلاءِ لَكانُوا كِفايَةً...
quote: (He said to the Imam:) O Hussain! I see your companions are few... Come with me to my tribe's mountains so I can bring you twenty thousand swordsmen.
